sanskrit:
पृथक्त्वेन तु यज्ज्ञानं नानाभावान्पृथग्विधान्।
वेत्ति सर्वेषु भूतेषु तज्ज्ञानं विद्धि राजसम्॥
transliteration:
pṛthaktvena tu yaj jñānaṃ nānā-bhāvān pṛthag-vidhān |
vetti sarveṣu bhūteṣu taj jñānaṃ viddhi rājasam ||
literal_meaning: But that knowledge which sees manifold, separate, and diverse entities in all beings—know that knowledge to be of the nature of passion.
contextual_meaning: A fragmented, hyper-individualistic worldview that treats every person as an isolated, competing unit; it fuels comparison, status anxiety, and the illusion that worth is body-bound and performance-driven.
psychological_state: Chronic comparison and identity diffusion—self-esteem rises and crashes with external metrics; underlying dread that if the body fails, the self ceases.
trigger_scenario: Scrolling LinkedIn at 2 a.m., noticing peers' promotions, feeling simultaneously superior and inferior, unable to sleep because 'I’m behind.'
anchor_text: See the same life in everyone; separation is the mind’s illusion.
